title: Plain-language glossary (terms defined on first use, cited)
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task drafts the plain-language glossary that pages link to on first use of a hard term; each definition is restated from a vetted source. Drafts are marked 'draft' and not published until dual sign-off. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Draft a plain-language, cited glossary defining key Ewing sarcoma terms on first use, at <= grade 8, for cross-linking from guide pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Plain-language definitions of key terms (biopsy, staging, metastatic, chemotherapy, radiation, EWSR1, remission, relapse, etc.), each cited; <= grade 8
Every definition restates a vetted source and carries provenance (passes citation-coverage); no model-generated medical facts
Marked draft, not published, until dual sign-off
